blogs.title: Customer Retention & Predictive Churn Analytics
blogs.excerpt:
Gemini said
VIZ-ly is an end-to-end predictive dashboard built with Power BI and Python. It diagnoses a critical 57% SaaS churn rate by identifying behavioral usage gaps and billing friction, transforming raw data into actionable retention strategies to save $697k in MRR.
blogs.content:
Stop Guessing Why Customers Leave: Building VIZ-ly, a Predictive Churn Dashboard
By [Saurav]

In the SaaS world, there is an old saying: Acquiring a new customer is five times more expensive than keeping an existing one. Yet, when I looked at how many companies handle customer churn, I noticed a recurring theme. They treat it like a post-mortem. They look at a spreadsheet at the end of the month, see that 500 people canceled, and ask, "What happened?"

I wanted to change that question to: "Who is about to leave, and how do we stop them today?"

That was the genesis of my latest data project: VIZ-ly, an end-to-end retention intelligence dashboard built with Python, Machine Learning, and Power BI. Here is how I built it, and the hidden revenue leaks I uncovered along the way.

The Problem: A $697k Monthly Bleed
For this project, I analyzed a dataset of over 2,800 subscription users. The baseline metrics were grim: the business was sitting on a 57.3% churn rate, putting roughly $697,000 in Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) at risk every single month.

If a Customer Success team tried to manually reach out to every single user, they would drown. They needed a targeted strategy. They needed to know exactly which behavioral red flags to look for.

Step 1: Moving Beyond SQL with Machine Learning
It’s easy to build a bar chart showing how many people left. It’s much harder to prove why.

Before I even opened Power BI, I loaded the raw data into Python. I used scikit-learn to train a Random Forest Classifier. My goal wasn't to deploy an AI bot; my goal was to extract Feature Importance. I wanted the math to tell me which user behaviors were actually driving the cancellations.

The algorithm returned two massive predictors: payment_failures and avg_weekly_usage_hours.

Step 2: The "Aha!" Moments
With the drivers identified, I used Python's seaborn library to perform Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) and map out the exact behavioral thresholds. The insights were staggering:

The 10-Hour Cliff: By mapping usage density, I discovered that retained users average around 15 hours of platform use a week. However, if a user drops below the 10-hour mark, they enter the "danger zone." Their probability of churning skyrockets.

The Billing Friction Catalyst: I found that a single payment failure is manageable, but the moment a user hits their second payment failure, churn probability jumps to 64%. People aren't necessarily leaving because they hate the product; they are leaving because their credit card expired and the billing system annoyed them.

The Premium Hemorrhage: While the "Basic" tier had the highest volume of cancellations, the "Premium" tier accounted for the vast majority of the actual financial loss.

Step 3: Designing the Command Center (Power BI)
Insights are useless if the business teams can't understand them. I needed to translate my Python findings into an operational tool.

I designed the VIZ-ly Dashboard in Power BI using a "Dark Mode" executive UI. I utilized the F-Pattern visual hierarchy:

The Top Row (The Pulse): High-contrast KPI cards showing Total Revenue at Risk and Current Churn %. A manager can understand the health of the business in 3 seconds.

The Middle Row (The Why): Visualizations of the usage density gaps and plan breakdowns.

The Bottom Row (The Action): A clear mapping of the payment failure risk, dictating exactly when intervention is needed.

I engineered dynamic DAX measures behind the scenes so that when a stakeholder clicks on the "Premium" plan, every financial metric and behavioral chart recalculates in milliseconds.

The Takeaway
Data analysis is not about making pretty charts; it is about driving business action.

By identifying the 10-hour engagement cliff and the billing friction points, the VIZ-ly dashboard gives the Product and Customer Success teams a clear roadmap. Instead of guessing, they can set up automated re-engagement emails for low-usage users and introduce payment grace periods—potentially saving hundreds of thousands of dollars in MRR.

blogs.title: VIZ-ly (A Dashboard Platform)
blogs.excerpt: AI Dashboard Builder is a full-stack data visualization web app built with React and FastAPI that allows users to upload datasets (CSV/Excel), preview data, and generate interactive charts. It features a drag-and-drop dashboard builder with resizable components and supports exporting dashboards as images or PDFs, making it a lightweight alternative to tools like Power BI and Tableau.
blogs.content:
📊 AI Dashboard Builder
Build Smart Data Dashboards with React & FastAPI 🚀

In today’s data-driven world, tools like Power BI and Tableau dominate business intelligence. But what if you could build your own version—customized, lightweight, and developer-friendly?

That’s exactly what I built: AI Dashboard Builder, a full-stack web application that transforms raw datasets into interactive dashboards in just a few clicks.

🧠 Project Overview

AI Dashboard Builder is a data visualization platform that allows users to:

Upload datasets (CSV / Excel)
Preview and understand data instantly
Generate interactive charts
Build fully customizable dashboards
Drag, resize, and rearrange components
Export dashboards as images or PDFs

It simplifies complex analytics workflows into a smooth and intuitive experience.

🎯 Key Features
📁 Dataset Upload

Upload .csv and .xlsx files with ease.
The backend processes data efficiently using Pandas.

🔍 Data Preview

Quickly inspect:

Dataset columns
Sample rows
Data structure

This helps users understand the dataset before visualization.

📈 Dynamic Chart Generation

Automatically generate charts such as:

📊 Bar Charts
📉 Line Charts
🥧 Pie Charts

Powered by Recharts, charts adapt dynamically based on your dataset.

🧩 Dashboard Builder

Create your own dashboard with:

Drag & drop functionality
Resizable components
Flexible layout system

Built using React Grid Layout for smooth interactions.

📤 Export Options

Download your dashboards as:

🖼 PNG Images
📄 PDF Reports

Perfect for presentations and reporting.

💾 Save Dashboard (Optional)
Save dashboard layouts
Retrieve them using a unique ID
Enables persistent analytics workflows
🏗️ Tech Stack
🔹 Frontend
React
React Router
Recharts
React Grid Layout
🔹 Backend
FastAPI
Pandas
Uvicorn
📂 Project Structure
viz-dashboard-platform/
│
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py
│   └── requirements.txt
│
├── frontend/
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   └── AutoChart.js
│   │   ├── pages/
│   │   │   ├── UploadDataset.js
│   │   │   ├── DataPreview.js
│   │   │   ├── Visualization.js
│   │   │   └── Dashboard.js
│   │   └── App.js
│
└── README.md
⚙️ Installation & Setup
🔹 Backend Setup
cd backend
pip install -r requirements.txt
uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000

📍 Runs on: http://localhost:8000

🔹 Frontend Setup
cd frontend
npm install
npm start

📍 Runs on: http://localhost:3000

🔗 API Endpoints
Method	Endpoint	Description
POST	/upload	Upload dataset
GET	/preview	Get dataset preview
POST	/save-dashboard	Save layout
GET	/dashboard/{id}	Load saved dashboard
🔄 Application Workflow
Upload Dataset
      ↓
Preview Data
      ↓
Generate Charts
      ↓
Build Dashboard
      ↓
Export / Save Dashboard
💡 Use Cases

This platform can be used for:

📊 Business analytics dashboards
🎓 Student data science projects
📈 Data visualization tools
💼 Developer portfolio projects
🔮 Future Enhancements

To make this even more powerful:

🤖 AI-based chart recommendations
🎨 Theme customization
🔐 User authentication
☁️ Cloud storage integration
📊 Advanced analytics (filters, grouping)
👨‍💻 About the Developer

Saurav
Data Analyst | Frontend & Backend Developer

⭐ Support & Contribution

If you like this project:

⭐ Star the repository
🍴 Fork it
🛠 Contribute
📜 License

This project is open-source and available under the MIT License.

✨ Final Thoughts

AI Dashboard Builder showcases how modern technologies like React and FastAPI can be combined to create powerful, scalable data tools.

It’s a step toward building your own custom Business Intelligence platform—tailored exactly to your needs.

💬 Open to feedback, collaboration, and opportunities—feel free to connect!

blogs.title: Cal-C
blogs.excerpt: A Flutter-based commercial calculator app with GST calculator, temperature converter, profit calculator, and shopkeeper notes using Provider and SharedPreferences.
blogs.content:
🧮 Flutter Calculator App
A Smart Business Utility App Built with Flutter

In today’s fast-paced world, having a simple calculator is not enough—especially for business users who need quick insights on taxes, profits, and conversions.

To solve this, I built a multi-functional commercial calculator app using Flutter, designed to handle real-world business and daily utility tasks in one place.

🚀 Project Overview

This is not just a basic calculator—it's a complete utility toolkit that combines multiple features into a single, easy-to-use mobile application.

The app is built with a focus on:

📱 Clean and intuitive UI/UX
⚡ Fast and responsive performance
🧠 Practical real-world functionality
✨ Key Features
🧮 1. Basic Calculator

Perform standard arithmetic operations with a smooth and responsive interface.

💰 2. GST Calculator

A must-have feature for business users:

Instantly calculate GST
Add or remove GST from amounts
Accurate and quick results
📈 3. Profit Calculator

Designed for small business decision-making:

Calculate profit and loss
Understand margins instantly
Helps in pricing and planning
🌡 4. Temperature Converter

Supports multiple unit conversions:

Celsius
Fahrenheit
Kelvin
📝 5. Shopkeeper Notes

A lightweight note-saving feature:

Save important business notes
Persistent storage using SharedPreferences
Useful for daily record-keeping
🏗️ Tech Stack
Technology	Purpose
Flutter	Cross-platform app development
Dart	Core development language
Provider	State management
SharedPreferences	Local data persistence

Flutter enables building high-performance apps for both Android and iOS using a single codebase.

📂 Project Structure
lib/
│
├── models/
├── providers/
├── screens/
│   ├── calculator_screen.dart
│   ├── gst_calculator.dart
│   ├── profit_calculator.dart
│   ├── temperature_converter.dart
│   └── notes_screen.dart
│
├── widgets/
└── main.dart
⚙️ Installation & Setup
1️⃣ Clone the Repository
git clone https://github.com/Sauravx4/flutter-calculator-project.git
cd flutter-calculator-project
2️⃣ Install Dependencies
flutter pub get
3️⃣ Run the App
flutter run
📱 Supported Platforms
✅ Android
✅ iOS
🔄 App Workflow
User Input  
   ↓  
Select Tool (Calculator / GST / Profit / Converter / Notes)  
   ↓  
Process Data  
   ↓  
Display Result / Save Data  
💡 Real-World Use Cases

This app is designed for:

🏪 Small business owners & shopkeepers
🎓 Students for quick calculations
📊 Daily productivity and utility tasks
💼 Portfolio demonstration for Flutter developers
🔮 Future Enhancements

To make the app even more powerful:

📊 Calculation history tracking
🌙 Dark mode support
☁️ Cloud sync for notes
📤 Export reports (PDF)
🔐 User authentication
👨‍💻 About the Developer

Saurav
Flutter Developer | Data Analyst | Full Stack Enthusiast

⭐ Support & Contribution

If you find this project useful:

⭐ Star the repository
🍴 Fork it
🛠 Contribute to improvements
📜 License

This project is licensed under the MIT License.

🙌 Acknowledgements
Flutter community
Open-source contributors
Inspiration from real-world business tools
✨ Final Thoughts

This project demonstrates how a simple idea like a calculator can evolve into a powerful business utility app when combined with the right features and technologies.

It’s a great example of building practical, user-focused applications using modern frameworks.

💬 Feel free to connect with me for feedback, collaboration, or opportunities!

blogs.title: Customer Behaviour Analysis
blogs.excerpt: A complete Data Analytics Project focused on understanding customer behaviour through data exploration, cleaning, analysis, and visualization using Python, SQL, Power BI, and Excel.
blogs.content:
🧠 Customer Behaviour Analysis
Turning Raw Data into Business Insights

In today’s data-driven world, understanding customer behaviour is not just an advantage—it’s a necessity. Businesses that leverage data effectively can optimize marketing strategies, improve customer retention, and boost revenue.

In this project, I performed a complete end-to-end data analytics workflow to uncover meaningful insights from customer data using tools like Python, SQL, Power BI, and Excel.

📊 Project Objective

The goal of this project was to:

Analyze customer purchasing patterns
Segment customers based on behavior
Identify high-value customers
Generate actionable insights for business decision-making
🧰 Tools & Technologies Used
Tool	Purpose
🐍 Python	Data cleaning, transformation, EDA, and visualization
🗃️ SQL	Data querying, aggregation, and segmentation
📈 Power BI	Interactive dashboards and storytelling
📊 Excel	Data validation, pivot tables, and quick analysis
📁 Project Architecture

The project follows a structured and scalable workflow:

Customer-Behaviour-Analysis/
│
├── data/
│   ├── raw/
│   └── cleaned/
│
├── notebooks/
│   ├── 01_data_cleaning.ipynb
│   ├── 02_exploratory_analysis.ipynb
│   └── 03_modeling.ipynb
│
├── sql/
│   └── customer_queries.sql
│
├── reports/
│   ├── PowerBI_Dashboard.pbix
│   └── Excel_Analysis.xlsx
│
├── visuals/
│   └── dashboard_preview.png
│
├── requirements.txt
└── README.md
🔍 Step-by-Step Approach
1️⃣ Data Collection

Customer transaction and demographic data were collected from databases and CSV files.

2️⃣ Data Cleaning & Transformation

Using Pandas, I:

Handled missing values and duplicates
Standardized inconsistent formats
Converted date columns for time-based analysis
3️⃣ Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

With Matplotlib and Seaborn, I explored:

Customer spending distribution
Product performance
Seasonal sales trends
Correlations between variables
4️⃣ SQL-Based Analysis

Using SQL, I:

Segmented customers based on purchase behavior
Created aggregated metrics using GROUP BY
Used JOINs and window functions for deeper insights
5️⃣ Data Visualization (Power BI)

Built an interactive dashboard featuring:

📈 Sales trends over time
👥 Customer segmentation (age, region, spending)
🛍️ Product category performance
💰 Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
6️⃣ Excel Reporting

Created:

Pivot tables
Summary dashboards
Business-friendly reports for stakeholders
📊 Key Insights

Here are some impactful findings from the analysis:

🔹 Pareto Principle (80/20 Rule)

Around 20% of customers contribute to ~75% of total revenue

🔹 High-Value Customer Segment

Customers aged 25–40 show the highest loyalty and repeat purchases

🔹 Top Product Categories

Electronics and Home Appliances dominate recurring sales

🔹 Regional Trends

Urban customers have significantly higher repeat purchase rates

💡 Business Recommendations

Based on the insights:

🎯 Focus marketing campaigns on high-value customers
🔁 Implement loyalty programs for repeat buyers
📍 Target urban regions for expansion strategies
🛒 Optimize inventory for top-performing categories
🚀 How to Run This Project
git clone https://github.com/yourusername/customer-behaviour-analysis.git
cd customer-behaviour-analysis
pip install -r requirements.txt
🧑‍💻 About Me

Saurav
Data Analyst | Python | SQL | Power BI | Excel

📧 sauravmourya54@gmail.com

🔗 LinkedIn | GitHub

✨ Final Thoughts

This project demonstrates how raw data can be transformed into actionable business insights through a structured analytics approach.

From cleaning messy datasets to building interactive dashboards, every step plays a crucial role in telling a meaningful data story.

💬 If you found this useful or have feedback, feel free to connect or reach out
